pattern: Rewriting the condition on the difference array nums1 minus nums2 turns it into counting earlier values not exceeding the current value plus diff, a classic order-statistics scan.
hints: Subtract the two arrays elementwise so each index carries a single number.
The inequality then says an earlier transformed value may exceed the later one by at most diff.
Process left to right and, for each new value, count how many previously inserted values are at most that value plus diff using a balanced order structure or a merge-sort count.

edge_inputs.description: no pair satisfies with negative diff
large positive diff makes all pairs count
large negative diff makes none count

stress.description: two random arrays of equal length with a random diff
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    a = [rng.randint(-10000, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    b = [rng.randint(-10000, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    diff = rng.randint(-10000, 10000)
    return [a, b, diff]
stress.note: equal lengths required; random diff exercises the full range of counts

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def numberOfPairs(self, nums1, nums2, diff):
        import bisect
        n = len(nums1)
        a = [nums1[i] - nums2[i] for i in range(n)]
        seen = []
        count = 0
        for j in range(n):
            count += bisect.bisect_right(seen, a[j] + diff)
            bisect.insort(seen, a[j])
        return count
